executiveSummary.summary: Solid market opportunity with differentiated positioning, but execution challenges are real. Your influencer marketing background is a strong asset, but the technical co-founder dependency and competitive landscape create meaningful risk. The validation is encouraging but shallow - 40 'I'd try this' responses don't predict paying customers.

marketSize.sam: $8-12B creator commerce tools (estimated 8-12% of creator economy revenue)
marketSize.som: $50-100M realistic 5-year market given positioning between Shopify and social platforms
marketSize.methodology: Based on creator economy reports and existing player revenues (Gumroad ~$200M GMV, Stan Store undisclosed but smaller)

marketAnalysis.positioning: The fastest way for creators to monetize their existing content with zero technical setup
marketAnalysis.timing: Good - creator monetization tools are maturing, but social commerce integration is still fragmented
gaps: True one-click setup from social content

valueProposition.coreProblem: Creators waste hours setting up complex e-commerce tools when they should be creating content
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Mid-tier creators (5K-100K followers) in lifestyle, education, and digital product niches
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Import existing social content to auto-generate storefronts with built-in creator collaboration
valueProposition.whyNow: Social platforms are pushing commerce but keeping creators locked in - independent tools with social integration are the escape hatch

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Freemium SaaS with transaction fees - land with free tier, expand with Pro features and transaction volume
businessModel.pricingFramework: 5% transaction fee is defensible if setup is truly effortless - creators pay for convenience. Pro tier at $19/mo needs clear ROI

unitEconomics.estimatedLTV: $400-600 (assuming $200 avg monthly GMV per creator, 18-month retention)

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Transaction fees scale naturally with creator success. Key constraint is payment processing costs and customer support

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Next.js frontend, Node.js/Python backend, Stripe Connect, social API integrations

buildVsBuy.rationale: Stripe Connect handles complexity and compliance
Core differentiator, existing tools too generic
APIs are accessible, custom logic needed

risks.description: Solo non-technical founder building technical product - high dependency on finding right co-founder
risks.mitigation: Start with no-code MVP to prove traction before recruiting technical co-founder

risks.description: Stan Store and Beacons have head start and funding - they could copy key features quickly
risks.mitigation: Focus on specific niche first, build network effects through creator collaboration features

risks.description: Creator economy hype may be cooling, funding environment tighter
risks.mitigation: Focus on profitability from day one, avoid venture-dependent growth model

risks.description: Social platform API dependencies create platform risk
risks.mitigation: Build multi-platform integrations, focus on creator-owned data

week1to4: Build no-code MVP with Bubble/Webflow + Stripe
Interview 20 creators about specific pain points
week5to8: Collect transaction data and user feedback
Start technical co-founder search through AngelList, founder communities
week9to12: Apply to accelerators as validation/network building

decisionAgenda.question: Should you build a no-code MVP first or wait for a technical co-founder?

options.tradeoff: Gain: faster validation, prove demand. Lose: technical debt, limited features
Gain: proper technical foundation. Lose: 3-6 months delay, opportunity cost

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Build no-code MVP first. Your market knowledge is perishable, and proving demand will help recruit better co-founders.

decisionAgenda.question: Which creator niche should you focus on initially?

options.tradeoff: Gain: high engagement, proven buying behavior. Lose: saturated market
Gain: digital product focus, recurring revenue. Lose: longer sales cycles
Gain: larger market. Lose: unfocused messaging, harder to optimize

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Focus on fitness/wellness creators. They have proven commerce behavior and your agency background likely gives you contacts here.

decisionAgenda.question: Is 5% transaction fee the right pricing strategy?

options.tradeoff: Gain: higher revenue per transaction. Lose: price sensitivity, competitive disadvantage
Gain: price competitiveness. Lose: need higher volume for sustainability
Gain: predictable revenue, creator-friendly. Lose: excludes smaller creators

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with 5% but offer clear value story around setup time saved. Test willingness to pay in MVP.
